title: Kubernetes Purpose, Architecture, Reconciliation, and Environment Evidence · Cluster Case: K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop
summary: A civic platform team must decide which availability problem Kubernetes can solve without mistaking orchestration for application correctness or infrastructure durability. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s declarative reconciliation loop increment in cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure.
Explain how API requests, admission, persisted desired state, watches, caches, and component status relate without treating any one cache as authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop · Theory K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop · Theory K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example team sees kubectl 1.36.2, an unfamiliar current context, a healthy API endpoint, and a Deployment whose desired replicas differ from observed status. Trace client, API server, admission, storage, controller, scheduler, kubelet, and runtime authority without treating reconciliation as application correctness. Competency probe: Trace desired state, observed state, controller decisions, idempotent actions, status updates, and eventual convergence through a changed failure. A defensible solution applies K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to cluster architecture and reconciliation evidence map.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-architecture-reconciliation-evidence-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-architecture-reconciliation-evidence-5
    artifact: architecture-evidence.yaml
    decision: worked k8s architecture reconciliation evidence 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      request-path: kubectl -> api-server -> admission -> etcd
      controller-watch: desired-vs-observed
      node-path: scheduler -> kubelet -> runtime
      convergence: status-condition
    verify: kubectl version && kubectl cluster-info && kubectl get --raw /readyz?verbose
    failure: Reject client-version-only evidence, direct-etcd application access, or a claim that reconciliation guarantees application correctness.
    repair: Capture current context, server APIs, component authority, desired state, observed status, and the remaining distribution-specific boundary.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Applying a manifest runs its YAML instructions once in top-to-bottom order. This fails the K8s Declarative Reconciliation Loop contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
